rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the core framing, including whether the claimed chemistry-agnostic and model-free contribution is conceptually coherent.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what to think of the passage centrally involves checking whether the reasoning from architecture and datasets to generality and deployment suitability holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes quantitative and transfer claims, so assessing whether reported results support those claims is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: Because the passage positions CycleSenseNet against conventional model-based and learning baselines, judging the claimed delta over prior work is centrally implicated.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should disclose uncertainty because only an excerpt is provided and no full methods, tables, or experimental protocol are available.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must guard against endorsing broad chemistry-agnostic or fleet-scale claims beyond what the excerpt substantiates.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually reviewing, rewriting, or executing the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: Battery health estimation has deployment implications, so responsibility includes avoiding unsupported advice that could affect downstream safety or reliability decisions.
rationale.RX.5: The assessment responsibility should remain traceable to claims and evidence explicitly present in the provided artifact.
